_global.text:
## Summary
Build the full Roommate Expense Splitter (auth, households, expenses, equal-split balances, settle-up, URL-driven history filters, demo seed, 375px-usable UI) on the scaffolded Next.js 15 App Router + Prisma + JWT monolith.

## Scope

### Files to modify
- `prisma/schema.prisma` — add `Household`, `Membership`, `Expense`, `ExpenseShare`, `Settlement` models (money as `Int` cents); keep `User`/`Role`.
- `lib/auth.ts` — add `hashPassword`/`verifyPassword` (bcryptjs), `getSessionFromCookie()`, `SESSION_COOKIE` constant; keep `signToken`/`verifyToken`.
- `prisma/seed.ts` — seed demo household (3 members, ~10 expenses, 1 settlement) using shared `hashPassword`; keep `SEED_CRED`/`SEED_CREDS_JSON` stdout lines.
- `app/layout.tsx` — global styles, mobile viewport meta, app metadata.
- `app/page.tsx` — redirect `/` → `/dashboard` (or `/login` if unauthenticated).
- `.pipeline/surface.json` — add all new routes, components, and `data-testid`s.
- `.colossus-acceptance.json` — set `ready_testid: "dashboard-main"`, add `expect_text: ["Balances"]`, keep reject signatures.
- `package.json` — add `bcryptjs` dep.

### Files to create
- `lib/prisma.ts` — Prisma client singleton (global-cached for dev HMR).
- `lib/session.ts` — `requireSession()`/`getCurrentUser()` server helpers reading the cookie.
- `lib/balances.ts` — pure functions: `equalSplit(amountCents, memberIds)` (remainder-cent distribution), `computeBalances(expenses, settlements)` → per-member net + pairwise owes.
- `app/globals.css` — mobile-first responsive styles (no horizontal scroll at 375px).
- Auth (public):
  - `app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`, `app/(auth)/signup/page.tsx` + `LoginForm`/`SignupForm` client components.
- App (guarded, route group with guard layout):
  - `app/(app)/layout.tsx` — server guard: verify session, load user + membership; redirect to `/login` or `/onboarding`; renders nav shell.
  - `app/(app)/onboarding/page.tsx` — create-household / join-by-code (client forms).
  - `app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx` — recent expenses + Balances section; reads `?modal=add-expense|settle`.
  - `app/(app)/history/page.tsx` — filtered list; reads `searchParams` `member`, `from`, `to`.
  - `app/(app)/settings/page.tsx` — household settings (name, join-code, members).
  - `app/(app)/expenses/[id]/page.tsx` — expense detail.
  - Components: `AddExpenseModal`, `SettleUpModal`, `BalancesSection`, `ExpenseList`, `HistoryFilters`, `Nav`, `EmptyState`.
- API route handlers (`app/api/**/route.ts`): `auth/signup`, `auth/login`, `auth/logout`, `households`, `households/join`, `expenses`, `expenses/[id]`, `settlements`.

### Dependencies
- `bcryptjs` (+ `@types/bcryptjs` dev) for password hashing. No other new deps (reuse `jsonwebtoken`, Prisma, Next).

### Integrations
- None (no third-party external services).

### Assumptions
- Session = JWT in an httpOnly `session` cookie (`SameSite=Lax`, `Secure` in prod). Route guards run in the `(app)` server layout (Node runtime), not Edge middleware, because `jsonwebtoken` needs Node crypto.
- Public routes: `/login`, `/signup`, `/api/health`, `/api/auth/*`. All `(app)` routes require a valid session; authenticated users without a household are redirected to `/onboarding`.
- One active household per user for v1 (first membership); multi-household deferred.
- Money stored as integer cents; equal split distributes remainder cents to the first members so shares sum exactly to the total.
- Balances: member net = Σ(paid) − Σ(own shares), then settlements applied (A→B payment reduces A-owes-B). Pairwise rendered as "X owes Y $Z".
- No admin role used (spec: plain users only); `Role` enum retained but all users seeded/created as `USER`.
- Server components read data via Prisma directly; mutations go through `/api/*` handlers called with `fetch` (matches stack glue).

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Data model
**File:** `prisma/schema.prisma`
**Change:** Add `Household { id, name, joinCode @unique, createdAt }`; `Membership { id, userId, householdId, joinedAt, @@unique([userId, householdId]) }`; `Expense { id, householdId, description, amountCents Int, payerId, createdAt }`; `ExpenseShare { id, expenseId, userId, shareCents Int }`; `Settlement { id, householdId, fromUserId, toUserId, amountCents Int, createdAt }`. Add relations + indexes on `householdId`. Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name init`.

### Step 2: Auth & session helpers
**File:** `lib/auth.ts`, `lib/prisma.ts`, `lib/session.ts`
**Change:** Add bcrypt `hashPassword`/`verifyPassword`; export `SESSION_COOKIE="session"`. `lib/prisma.ts` exports a singleton client. `lib/session.ts` adds `getCurrentUser()` (reads cookie → `verifyToken` → Prisma user + membership) and `requireSession()` (redirect if absent).

### Step 3: Auth API routes
**File:** `app/api/auth/{signup,login,logout}/route.ts`
**Change:** `signup` — validate email/password, reject duplicates, `hashPassword`, create user, sign JWT, set httpOnly cookie. `login` — verify credentials, set cookie. `logout` — clear cookie. Return JSON with next redirect target.

### Step 4: Auth pages
**File:** `app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`, `app/(auth)/signup/page.tsx`
**Change:** Server pages rendering client forms that POST to the auth APIs and redirect to `/dashboard` (or `/onboarding`). Show inline errors. `data-testid`s on forms/inputs/submit.

### Step 5: Guarded app layout + onboarding
**File:** `app/(app)/layout.tsx`, `app/(app)/onboarding/page.tsx`, `app/api/households/{route.ts,join/route.ts}`
**Change:** Layout calls `getCurrentUser()`; redirect to `/login` if no session, `/onboarding` if no membership. Renders responsive `Nav`. `POST /api/households` creates household + membership, generates unique `joinCode`, returns it. `POST /api/households/join` validates code, creates membership. Onboarding page shows create + join forms and displays the join-code after creation.

### Step 6: Expenses & settlements API
**File:** `app/api/expenses/route.ts`, `app/api/expenses/[id]/route.ts`, `app/api/settlements/route.ts`
**Change:** `GET /api/expenses` — filter by `member`/`from`/`to`, scoped to caller's household. `POST /api/expenses` — validate, compute `equalSplit`, create `Expense` + `ExpenseShare`s in a transaction. `GET /api/expenses/[id]` — detail with shares. `POST /api/settlements` — create settlement between two members. All authorize household membership.

### Step 7: Dashboard + Balances
**File:** `app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx`, `components/BalancesSection.tsx`, `components/ExpenseList.tsx`, `components/EmptyState.tsx`
**Change:** Server page loads household expenses/settlements/members via Prisma, computes balances with `lib/balances.ts`. Renders `main data-testid="dashboard-main"`, recent `ExpenseList` (`data-testid="expense-list"`, empty state `data-testid="expenses-empty"`), and Balances section with heading text literally `Balances` and `data-testid="balances-heading"`. Reads `?modal=add-expense|settle` to render `AddExpenseModal`/`SettleUpModal`.

### Step 8: Add-expense & settle-up modals
**File:** `components/AddExpenseModal.tsx`, `components/SettleUpModal.tsx`
**Change:** Client components opened via `?modal=` query param (URL-addressable), closed by clearing the param. Add-expense: description, amount (dollars→cents), payer select, member checkboxes (equal split). Settle-up: from/to member + amount. POST to APIs, then `router.refresh()`.

### Step 9: History page with URL filters
**File:** `app/(app)/history/page.tsx`, `components/HistoryFilters.tsx`
**Change:** Server page reads `searchParams` (`member`, `from`, `to`), queries filtered expenses, renders list. `HistoryFilters` client component pushes selections to the URL query string so reloads reproduce the view. Expense rows link to `/expenses/[id]`.

### Step 10: Settings & expense detail
**File:** `app/(app)/settings/page.tsx`, `app/(app)/expenses/[id]/page.tsx`
**Change:** Settings shows household name, join-code (copyable), member list. Expense detail shows description, amount, payer, per-member shares. Both server-rendered, membership-authorized.

### Step 11: Seed
**File:** `prisma/seed.ts`
**Change:** Create 3 demo users (shared `hashPassword`), one household with join-code + 3 memberships, ~10 expenses with equal-split shares, and 1 settlement. Keep `SEED_CRED`/`SEED_CREDS_JSON` stdout lines with working passwords.

### Step 12: Styling, manifests, entrypoint
**File:** `app/globals.css`, `app/layout.tsx`, `.pipeline/surface.json`, `.colossus-acceptance.json`
**Change:** Mobile-first CSS usable at 375px (no horizontal scroll). Add viewport meta. Update `surface.json` with all routes/components/testids and `.colossus-acceptance.json` (`ready_testid: "dashboard-main"`, `expect_text: ["Balances"]`). Ensure deploy runs `prisma migrate deploy` + seed before `node server.js`.

## Testing Strategy
- **Unit** (`lib/balances.ts`): equal split with even/odd amounts sums to total; pairwise balances; settlement reduces A→B balance by exact amount.
- **API**: signup→login sets cookie; unauthenticated `/api/expenses` rejected; create household returns join-code; join adds member; add expense creates correct shares; settlement updates balance.
- **E2E/Playwright** (via surface.json testids): signup→create household→add expense→see it in recent list; Balances heading present with testid; filter history by member → URL reflects filter → reload reproduces view; seeded creds log in and show seeded dashboard.
- **Mobile**: render dashboard at 375px — nav + Balances visible, no horizontal scroll.
- **Seed**: run `npx prisma db seed`, assert `SEED_CREDS_JSON` on stdout and those creds authenticate.

## Risks
- Edge vs Node runtime: guards must stay in server components/route handlers (Node) since `jsonwebtoken` won't run on Edge middleware.
- Seed/app hash mismatch would break demo login — mitigated by the single shared `hashPassword` helper.
- Remainder-cent rounding in splits — covered by `equalSplit` unit tests ensuring shares sum to the total.
- Keep `surface.json`/`.colossus-acceptance.json` in sync or the render gate and generated tests target the stale stub.
